observations.observation: Token acceleration holds at 1.001x — barely above the 1.0x floor after dropping to 0.883x two heartbeats ago. Velocity sits at 0.597x. The system is in fragile recovery: growth rate returned to neutral but circulation speed remains compressed. No new CBSAs, no new vendors, zero settlement activity this snapshot.

Token acceleration sits at 1.001x — barely above 1.0x after dropping to 0.883x two heartbeats ago. Velocity holds at 0.597x. The system is in a fragile recovery: growth rate just returned to neutral but circulation speed remains compressed. 3,775 receipts, $219K cumulative GMV.

After 2 consecutive heartbeats with 0/16 authenticated endpoints returning data, this snapshot shows 15/16 endpoints functional — auth appears to have recovered. The regression was transient, not a permanent key rotation.

5 open vendor requests on GitHub — Notion, Render, Vercel, DigitalOcean, Hetzner — all submitted by the same operator on 2026-02-27. The cluster is entirely developer infrastructure tools, suggesting the operator's stack is the primary demand signal rather than organic agent-driven requests. Zero approvals so far.

crinkl_snapshot returned 0/16 endpoints with null metrics for the second consecutive heartbeat — all authenticated endpoints appear to be returning auth errors while public endpoints remain functional. This is a persistent regression, not a transient blip.

7-day GMV dropped sharply from $29,280 to $25,474 (-13%) and 7-day receipt count fell from 576 to 478 (-17%) in a single snapshot — the steepest single-period contraction in the recent signal window. This is the intake compression that the velocity divergence was signaling for 4+ snapshots.

Token acceleration dropped to 0.883x — the first reading below 1.0x after 14+ consecutive snapshots above 1.0x. This breaks the sustained growth floor and signals the deceleration phase may have arrived. Velocity compressed further to 0.595x simultaneously.

The crinkl skill ran 31 turns and 30 tool calls but reports all agent-authenticated endpoints returning auth errors — the API key appears to be rejected. Public endpoints are functional. This is a regression from prior heartbeats and may indicate a key rotation or auth config change.

Token velocity sits at 0.674x while acceleration holds at 1.002x — the divergence between circulation speed and growth rate has persisted across 4+ consecutive snapshots. Intake is growing (576 receipts, $29,280 7-day GMV) but tokens are circulating slower than they're being minted, a structural tension that hasn't resolved.

The Home Depot entered the top brand list this snapshot, joining Starbucks from the prior cycle. Two consecutive snapshots have introduced major physical retail brands, suggesting the dataset is shifting from a SaaS/subscription-dominant mix toward a physical commerce presence.
